SEARCH
Package com.icarus.events

Class OrganizerManageEventTest

java.lang.Object
com.icarus.events.OrganizerManageEventTest
public class OrganizerManageEventTest extends Object
Instrumented UI tests for OrganizerManageEventActivity.

User Stories Tested: US 02.01.01 As an organizer I want to create a new public event and generate a unique promotional QR code that links to the event description and event poster in the app. US 02.01.02 As an organizer, I want to create a private event that is not visible on the event listing and does not generate a promotional QR code US 02.01.03 As an organizer, I want to invite specific entrants to a private event’s waiting list by searching via name, phone number and/or email. US 02.04.02 As an organizer I want to update an event poster to provide visual information to entrants. US 02.05.02 As an organizer I want to set the system to sample a specified number of attendees to register for the event. US 02.05.03 As an organizer I want to be able to draw a replacement applicant from the pooling system when a previously selected applicant cancels or rejects the invitation. US 02.09.01 As an organizer, I want to assign an entrant as a co-organizer for my event, which prevents them from joining the entrant pool for that event.

Tests use temporary Firestore collections to avoid affecting production data.

  • Constructor Details

    • OrganizerManageEventTest

      public OrganizerManageEventTest()

  • Method Details

    • setup

      public void setup() throws InterruptedException
      Sets up Firestore test data before each test.

      Overrides Firestore collections to test versions, adds a sample event with ID createdEventId, adds entrants with different statuses ("waiting", "selected", "rejected", "registered"), creates user documents to hold entrant names. Also creates user documents to which are not part of the event

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if Firestore operations are interrupted

    • testQRCodeCreated

      public void testQRCodeCreated() throws InterruptedException
      Test to see if the quick share menu appears when creating a QR code and the event is public

      Launches the OrganizerManageEventActivity activity, clicks the Share QR code, and generates the QR code

      User Story Tested: US 02.01.01 As an organizer I want to create a new public event and generate a unique promotional QR code that links to the event description and event poster in the app.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if Firestore reads or UI operations are interrupted

    • testQRCodeFailedToShare

      public void testQRCodeFailedToShare() throws InterruptedException
      Test to see if the quick share menu appears when creating a QR code and the event is private

      Launches the OrganizerManageEventActivity activity, clicks the Share QR code, and verifies that a toast message is produced

      User Story Tested: US 02.01.02 As an organizer, I want to create a private event that is not visible on the event listing and does not generate a promotional QR code

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if Firestore reads or UI operations are interrupted

    • testInviteEntrant

      public void testInviteEntrant() throws InterruptedException
      Test to invite an entrant to a private entrant

      Launches the OrganizerManageEventActivity activity, clicks the invite specific entrant button, and verifies that the ListView displays the entrants not in waiting list, oragnizers, or admins. Entrant on list is then invited to the event with status selected

      User Story Tested: US 02.01.03 As an organizer, I want to invite specific entrants to a private event’s waiting list by searching via name, phone number and/or email.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if Firestore reads or UI operations are interrupted

    • testUpdateImage

      public void testUpdateImage() throws Exception
      Tests to upload a new image and verify it exists in cloudinary and firestore

      Launches the OrganizerManageEventActivity activity, clicks the "Update Poster" button, creates a blank image, uploads that image. verifies the image is in the imageview, and in the database. Written by Claude, April 5, 2026 "I need to create a test image. click the upload poster button, check that the image is uploaded to cloudinary, check the imageview displays the image, afterwards I need to remove the image from cloudinary the OrganizerCreateEventTest.java files does this. But has too many steps for what I need to do in this test"

      User Story Tested: US 02.04.02 As an organizer I want to update an event poster to provide visual information to entrants.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if Firestore reads or UI operations are interrupted
      Exception

    • testSampleAttendees

      public void testSampleAttendees() throws InterruptedException
      Test to sample a single entrant from the waiting list

      Launches the OrganizerManageEventActivity activity, clicks the "sample attendees" button, and sets the number to 1. and samples 1 attendee. Entrant Zero is then added to the selected status list.

      User Story Tested: US 02.05.02 As an organizer I want to set the system to sample a specified number of attendees to register for the event.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if Firestore reads or UI operations are interrupted

    • testReplacedDeclined

      public void testReplacedDeclined() throws InterruptedException
      Test to replace cancelled entrants in an event's entrant subcollection with entrants in the waiting list

      Launches the OrganizerManageEventActivity activity, clicks the "replaced declined" button, and verifies entrant zero is sampled

      User Story Tested: US 02.05.03 As an organizer I want to be able to draw a replacement applicant from the pooling system when a previously selected applicant cancels or rejects the invitation.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if Firestore reads or UI operations are interrupted

    • testAddOrganizer

      public void testAddOrganizer() throws InterruptedException
      Test to add a entrant to the organizer array of an event

      Launches the OrganizerManageEventActivity activity, clicks the "Add Organizers" button, and selects a user from the list and verfies they are added to the event as an organizer

      User Story Tested: US 02.09.01 As an organizer, I want to assign an entrant as a co-organizer for my event, which prevents them from joining the entrant pool for that event.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if Firestore reads or UI operations are interrupted

    • cleanup

      public void cleanup() throws InterruptedException
      Removes test data created during the test run from Firestore.

      Deletes the test entrants from the event's entrants subcollection, removes the corresponding user documents, deletes any notifications associated with the created event, and finally deletes the event document itself.

      This method is executed after each test to ensure the database is cleaned up and does not contain leftover test data.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the cleanup wait is interrupted
